name: coding-status-for-discord
description: Shows what you're working on — language, session time, and a rotating spinner word — as your Discord Rich Presence.

claudeSpinner.enabled.description: Master switch. When off, disconnects from Discord and shows no presence.

claudeSpinner.cycleSpeed.description: How often the spinner word rotates, in seconds. Minimum 5 respects Discord's presence rate limit.

claudeSpinner.cycleWords.description: Rotate words on the cycle interval. When off, picks one word on activation and keeps it.

claudeSpinner.customWords.description: Extra words to mix into the spinner. Each entry 1–128 characters.

claudeSpinner.showLanguage.description: Show a 'Working in X' line below the cycling word.

claudeSpinner.showWorkspace.description: Append the workspace folder name to the status line. Off by default for privacy.

claudeSpinner.showElapsedTime.description: Show elapsed session time on your Discord profile.

claudeSpinner.showLanguageIcon.description: Show a language-specific icon as a small overlay. Falls back to the Claude logo when no icon is mapped.

claudeSpinner.smartState.description: Vary the status line when you're debugging, reviewing a diff, or focused on the terminal.

enumDescriptions: Quadruple the cycle interval while idle (clamped to 120 seconds).
Stop cycling, keep the last presence visible.
Stop cycling and clear the presence from Discord.
claudeSpinner.idleBehavior.description: What happens when VS Code loses focus past the idle threshold.

claudeSpinner.idleThresholdMinutes.description: Minutes of VS Code inactivity before idle mode engages.

claudeSpinner.wordRarity.description: Opt-in weighted random: common (~70%), uncommon (~25%), rare (~5%).

claudeSpinner.timeBasedPools.description: Opt-in: bias words toward early-session (warming up), mid-session (in the zone), and long-session (deep session) pools.
